API and Coordinates

Source: vignettes/api-and-coordinates.Rmd
api-and-coordinates.Rmd

Rsassy keeps the R surface small and close to the Rust API.

Main functions

  • sassy_search() is the convenience wrapper for one-off calls.
  • sassy_searcher() creates a reusable searcher.
  • sassy_searcher_search() searches with a reusable searcher.
  • sassy_search_connection() streams from an R connection.
  • sassy_features() reports selected backend and CPU/build features.
  • sassy_set_backend() requests a backend before the first native call.

Coordinates

Match coordinates are 0-based and half-open:

  • text_start, text_end describe the interval in the input text.
  • pattern_start, pattern_end describe the interval in the input pattern.
  • pattern_idx, text_idx are 0-based indices into vector/list inputs.

This mirrors the Rust crate and avoids repeated conversion at the native boundary.

matches <- sassy_search("ACGT", "TTACGTAA", k = 0, alphabet = "dna", rc = FALSE)
matches[, c("text_start", "text_end", "pattern_start", "pattern_end", "cigar")]

Input types

pattern and text can be character vectors, raw vectors, or lists of raw vectors / character scalars. Use raw vectors when input is already byte-oriented and should not pass through R string encoding.

sassy_search(charToRaw("ACGT"), charToRaw("TTACGTAA"), k = 0, alphabet = "dna")

Match regions

Set match_region = TRUE to include the matched text interval. Reverse-strand regions are reverse-complemented so the region and CIGAR are in the input pattern direction.

sassy_search(
  "ATCGATCG",
  "GGGGATCGATCGTTTT",
  k = 1,
  alphabet = "dna",
  match_region = TRUE
)

Search modes

mode = "single" searches each pattern/text pair independently. mode = "batch_texts", mode = "batch_patterns", and mode = "encoded_patterns" expose Sassy’s batched paths where applicable. The batched pattern modes currently require equal pattern byte lengths and the IUPAC profile.

On this page

Developed by Sounkou Mahamane Toure.
